So in code:

class Solution {
public:
    string longestPalindrome(string s) {
        if (s.empty()) return "";
        int n = s.length();
        int start = 0, maxLen = 0;
        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) {
            // Check odd case
            int l1 = i, r1 = i;
            while (l1 >= 0 && r1 < n && s[l1] == s[r1]) {
                l1--;
                r1++;
            }
            int len1 = r1 - l1 - 1;
            if (len1 > maxLen) {
                maxLen = len1;
                start = l1 + 1;
            }
            
            // Check even case
            int l2 = i, r2 = i + 1;
            while (l2 >= 0 && r2 < n && s[l2] == s[r2]) {
                l2--;
                r2++;
            }
            int len2 = r2 - l2 - 1;
            if (len2 > maxLen) {
                maxLen = len2;
                start = l2 + 1;
            }
        }
        return s.substr(start, maxLen);
    }
};

even case: l2=1, r2=2. s[1] and s[2] are both 'b's. So loop runs. l2-- to 0, r2++ to 3. Check s[0] (c) vs s[3] (d) — no. So len2 is 3-0-1=2. So maxLen is 2, start is 0+1=1. So substring is s.substr(1,2) which is "bb". 

Yes.

Time complexity: For each i, we do O(n) work in the while loops. So total O(n^2) time. For n up to 1000, this is acceptable. 

Space complexity: O(1), since we are using only variables. 

The energy implications are low since it's O(n^2) time and O(1) space.

solution: 
```cpp
class Solution {
public:
    string longestPalindrome(string s) {
        if (s.empty()) return "";
        int n = s.length();
        int start = 0, maxLen = 0;
        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) {
            // Check odd-length palindromes
            int l1 = i, r1 = i;
            while (l1 >= 0 && r1 < n && s[l1] == s[r1]) {
                l1--;
                r1++;
            }
            int len1 = r1 - l1 - 1;
            if (len1 > maxLen) {
                maxLen = len1;
                start = l1 + 1;
            }
            
            // Check even-length palindromes
            int l2 = i, r2 = i + 1;
            while (l2 >= 0 && r2 < n && s[l2] == s[r2]) {
                l2--;
                r2++;
            }
            int len2 = r2 - l2 - 1;
            if (len2 > maxLen) {
                maxLen = len2;
                start = l2 + 1;
            }
        }
        return s.substr(start, maxLen);
    }
};
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n^2)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: Expands around each character as a potential palindrome center (both odd and even length cases). Time complexity is O(n^2) due to nested expansion loops, but avoids unnecessary checks by early termination. Space is constant. Efficient for n ≤ 1000.
